assets: deep operational knowledge of denial and prior auth failure modes
ability to translate messy workflows into structured logic
ability to synthesize public payer rules into usable operator outputs
distribution: direct access to employer customers is not usable
network_access: potential access to non-employer RCM operators and health-tech buyers
constraints: must avoid employer-confidential information and internal materials
credibility_signals: practical understanding of denial and prior auth economics
unfair_advantages: strong judgment on which denial and prior auth issues actually matter operationally
ability to package niche workflow knowledge into AI-assisted operator tools
clean problem selection in a painful, expensive niche

binding_constraints: obvious provider-facing opportunities carry PHI and workflow liability risk
employer customer overlap and confidentiality boundaries reduce usable buyer access
4 hours per week plus no consulting and no sales calls makes fast revenue unlikely
the business must be built only from public sources, synthetic examples, and clean-room analysis

opportunities.title: Async denial and prior-auth workflow teardown for small RCM firms
opportunities.thesis: Sell fixed-scope workflow teardowns using public rules and synthetic examples to independent billing and RCM firms.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user understands margin leaks and workflow bottlenecks better than generalist consultants or generic AI builders.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: It still looks like consulting, usually needs calls, and conflicts with the user's stated preference.
validation_plan: Offer one fixed-scope teardown product page to non-employer RCM firms only.
Send 20 targeted async messages and track reply and purchase intent.
Only continue if at least 2 buyers agree to a paid async engagement without calls.
kill_criteria: Stop if buyers insist on live calls or custom consulting.
Stop if legal review suggests the niche overlaps too closely with employer restrictions.

opportunities.title: Public-rule denial intelligence subscription for RCM operators
opportunities.thesis: Turn public payer policy changes and prior-auth rule updates into a paid operator-ready feed, taxonomy, and playbook subscription.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user can identify which public rule changes will actually produce denials and rework, which is the valuable layer buyers pay for.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: Distribution is weak and the product fails if it relies on employer-derived knowledge or looks like commodity summaries.
validation_plan: Create 3 sample artifacts from public sources only.
Launch a landing page with a founding subscription offer.
Seek 2 paid preorders or 5 refundable deposits from non-employer buyers within 7 days.
kill_criteria: Stop if buyers do not pay for the information layer.
Stop if valuable output cannot be created entirely from public sources and synthetic examples.

opportunities.title: Template and playbook library for denial and prior-auth operations
opportunities.thesis: Sell SOPs, checklists, dashboards, and prompt packs as an async digital product for RCM operators.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user can create templates grounded in real workflow friction instead of generic administrative templates.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: It is easier to copy, less recurring, and may not justify ongoing subscription revenue.
validation_plan: Package one high-value starter bundle for one specialty.
List it on a landing page and one relevant marketplace if appropriate.
Continue only if at least 3 buyers purchase the starter pack within 14 days.
kill_criteria: Stop if buyers treat it as a one-time low-value purchase with no renewal intent.

opportunities.title: Denial intelligence library to benchmark feed to lightweight ops software
opportunities.thesis: Start with a paid information and template subscription, then add benchmark data and software only after repeated paid demand reveals a stable product surface.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user can turn specialized workflow judgment into a structured ontology and later software without touching PHI-heavy execution at the start.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: This is slower than services and software should not be built if the information layer does not pull buyers first.
validation_plan: Review employment and IP boundaries before any build.
Launch a founding subscription with 3 proof artifacts and a preorder path.
Continue to software only if at least 3 paying customers request repeated searchable workflows or alerts.
kill_criteria: Stop if fewer than 2 paid preorders or 5 deposits arrive in the first 7-14 days.
Stop if maintaining the service exceeds 4 hours per week before 3 customers.
Stop if legal review raises unresolved IP, confidentiality, or non-solicit issues.

rejected_paths.title: Provider-facing AI copilot for denials or prior auth
rejected_paths.reason: Too likely to require PHI handling, substantive workflow liability, and overlap with employer-sensitive customer relationships.

rejected_paths.title: Broad healthcare AI consulting
rejected_paths.reason: Conflicts with the user's no-consulting and no-sales-call constraints and does not compound into an asset efficiently at 4 hours per week.

rejected_paths.title: Pure content business about revenue cycle management
rejected_paths.reason: Too slow without an existing audience and too weakly differentiated as a primary monetization path.

next_actions: Review employment agreement and create a written clean-room boundary memo.
Pick one narrow ICP and one specialty cluster.
Create 3 sample artifacts from public sources only.
Launch a landing page with a founding subscription offer and payment path.
Run 30 targeted async outreaches to non-employer buyers only.
Continue only if at least 2 paid preorders or 5 deposits arrive without legal concerns.
